purpose.primary_objective: Provide consistent economic categorisation of SME bank transactions to support explainable credit analytics, monitoring, and scoring for UK businesses.
design_principles: Flat economic categories optimised for credit metrics, not nested P&L hierarchy
One transaction maps to one category only - no double counting
Clear separation of trading income from non-trading inflows
People costs, finance costs, and transport isolated for intensity metrics
Balance sheet movements excluded from operating margin calculations
Transaction direction (DEBIT/CREDIT) explicit for each category
Every category includes synthetic prompt guidance for LLM-generated test data
UK market specific: HMRC, VAT, UK merchants, UK payment rails

high.description: Exact merchant match, unambiguous pattern, known counterparty

medium.description: Semantic match, probable intent, contextual inference

low.description: Ambiguous description, unclear intent, requires manual review

classification_guidance.fallback_strategy: Assign to UNCATEGORISED with confidence score below 0.60. Flag for manual review in credit assessment. Exclude from automated metric calculations.

revenue.formula: SUM(REVENUE categories where transaction_direction = CREDIT) - SUM(refunds_issued)
revenue.description: Total trading income from core business activities, net of refunds and chargebacks

non_revenue_inflows.description: Inflows that are not trading revenue and should be excluded from turnover

cogs.description: Direct costs to produce or deliver goods and services sold

gross_profit.description: Profit after direct production costs, before operating expenses

gross_margin.description: Gross profit as a percentage of revenue, indicator of pricing power and production efficiency

interpretation.warning: < 20% suggests pricing pressure or high input costs

total_operating_costs.description: Total overhead and operational spend excluding COGS

ebitda.description: Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation and Amortisation. Proxy for operational cash generation.
ebitda.notes: Interest is excluded (captured in FINANCE_COSTS but not deducted). Tax is excluded (TAX_AND_STATUTORY category). Depreciation/amortisation not visible in transaction data.

ebitda_margin.description: EBITDA as a percentage of revenue, core operational profitability metric

interpretation.warning: 0-5% suggests low profitability or growth investment phase

people_intensity.description: Total people costs as a percentage of revenue, indicator of labour dependency and automation level

finance_cost_burden.description: Finance and banking costs as a percentage of revenue, includes payment processing and borrowing costs

interpretation.elevated: 3-5% may indicate high card volumes or expensive financing
interpretation.warning: > 5% suggests high debt servicing or inefficient payment infrastructure

operating_expense_ratio.formula: SUM(OPERATING_EXPENSES excluding PEOPLE_COSTS, FINANCE_COSTS, TRANSPORT_AND_LOGISTICS, INSURANCE) / revenue
operating_expense_ratio.description: Core operating expenses as percentage of revenue, excludes people and finance costs for cleaner overhead analysis

fixed_cost_ratio.description: Proportion of operating costs that are fixed/committed, indicator of operational leverage and flexibility

interpretation.high_fixed: > 60% suggests lower flexibility but potential economies of scale
interpretation.variable_heavy: < 40% indicates flexible cost base, easier to scale down

debt_service_coverage.description: Ability to service debt from operating cash flow. Critical lender metric.

interpretation.warning: 1.0-1.25x tight coverage, vulnerable to revenue shocks
interpretation.distressed: < 1.0x unable to service debt from operations

working_capital_burn.description: Net capital consumed or generated excluding operating activity, indicator of liquidity stress or growth investment

interpretation.positive_burn: Consuming capital faster than generating it, may indicate distress or growth phase
interpretation.negative_burn: Generating capital, indicates strong cash generation or deleveraging

revenue_concentration_risk.formula: Computed from frequency and clustering of revenue receipts
revenue_concentration_risk.description: Derived signal: high concentration in few large receipts vs distributed small receipts
revenue_concentration_risk.notes: Requires transaction-level frequency analysis, not a simple sum

payment_stress_signals.formula: COUNT(failed_payments) + COUNT(overdraft_interest) + patterns in late supplier payments
payment_stress_signals.description: Composite signal of liquidity stress from failed transactions and timing patterns
payment_stress_signals.notes: Behavioral metric derived from ADJUSTMENTS category and payment timing

supplier_dependency.description: Concentration risk in supply chain, requires counterparty analysis
supplier_dependency.notes: Requires transaction-level counterparty clustering, not category-level

REVENUE.name: Revenue
REVENUE.definition: Trading income from core business activities. Funds received for goods or services sold.

subcategories.name: Card Payments
subcategories.definition: Revenue received via card acquiring services including card scheme settlements, POS terminal receipts, and payment facilitator payouts. Typically batched and settled on a regular cadence.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK card payment settlements from acquirers. Use merchant names like: Worldpay, Barclaycard, Stripe, Square, SumUp, Zettle. Include patterns: 'WORLDPAY SETTLEMENT', 'STRIPE PAYOUT', 'BARCLAYCARD MERCHANT', 'SQUARE INC', 'SUMUP PAYMENTS'. Amounts: £500-£15,000 for retail, £50-£5,000 for small businesses. Frequency: daily or weekly batches. Description should include settlement date or batch reference.

subcategories.name: Bank Transfers In
subcategories.definition: Direct customer payments received via bank transfer for goods or services, including invoice settlements and ad-hoc customer payments.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK bank transfers from customers. Use payment types: 'FASTER PAYMENT', 'BACS', 'CHAPs'. Include patterns: 'FP FROM [CUSTOMER NAME]', 'BACS CREDIT', 'CUSTOMER PAYMENT', 'INVOICE 12345'. Customer names should be mix of: business names (ABC LTD, XYZ SERVICES), personal names (J SMITH, SARAH JONES). Amounts: £100-£25,000. Include invoice references occasionally: 'INV-2024-001', 'REF 45678'. Frequency: irregular, clustered around month-end.

subcategories.name: Platform Payouts
subcategories.definition: Revenue settlements from online marketplaces, platforms, or intermediaries that collect customer funds and remit proceeds to the business.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK marketplace and platform payouts. Platform names: Amazon, eBay, Etsy, Shopify, Deliveroo, Uber Eats, Just Eat. Patterns: 'AMAZON EU SARL', 'PAYPAL TRANSFER', 'EBAY PAYMENT', 'SHOPIFY PAYMENTS', 'DELIVEROO PAYMENTS', 'UBER EATS TRANSFER'. Amounts: £200-£8,000 for food delivery, £500-£20,000 for ecommerce. Frequency: weekly or bi-weekly settlements. Include settlement IDs occasionally.

subcategories.name: Cash Deposits
subcategories.definition: Cash deposited into the business account, typically representing daily takings or cash sales from physical retail or hospitality operations.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK cash deposits. Patterns: 'CASH DEPOSIT', 'BRANCH DEPOSIT', 'ATM DEPOSIT', 'COUNTER DEPOSIT [BRANCH NAME]'. Branch names: realistic UK locations like 'DEPOSIT BARCLAYS LEEDS', 'HSBC MANCHESTER BRANCH', 'LLOYDS BIRMINGHAM'. Amounts: £100-£2,500 for small retail, £500-£5,000 for hospitality. Frequency: daily for hospitality/retail, weekly for others. Include till references occasionally: 'TILL 1', 'DAILY TAKINGS'.

subcategories.name: Other Revenue
subcategories.definition: Operating income related to trading but not direct sales receipts, such as commissions earned, service fees charged, or minor reimbursements related to trading activity.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate miscellaneous UK trading receipts. Patterns: 'COMMISSION RECEIVED', 'SERVICE FEE', 'REFERRAL FEE', 'AFFILIATE PAYMENT', 'CONSULTING FEE', 'LICENSE FEE'. Amounts: £50-£3,000. Frequency: irregular. Include reference codes occasionally. Should be clearly business-related but not direct product/service sales.

NON_REVENUE_INFLOWS.name: Non-Revenue Inflows
NON_REVENUE_INFLOWS.definition: Inflows that are not trading revenue and must be excluded from turnover calculations. Includes grants, subsidies, insurance claims, refunds received, and asset sale proceeds.

subcategories.name: Grants and Subsidies
subcategories.definition: Government grants, subsidies, and support payments received by the business including COVID support schemes, R&D grants, local authority grants.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK government grant receipts. Patterns: 'HMRC GRANT', 'INNOVATE UK', 'LOCAL AUTHORITY GRANT', 'BOUNCE BACK LOAN SCHEME', 'SMALL BUSINESS GRANT', 'R&D TAX CREDIT', 'SCOTTISH ENTERPRISE', 'WELSH GOVERNMENT'. Amounts: £1,000-£50,000. Frequency: one-off or quarterly. Include scheme names and reference numbers.

subcategories.name: Insurance Claims Received
subcategories.definition: Insurance claim payments received from insurers for business losses, damage, or covered events. Typically irregular and non-trading.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK insurance claim receipts. Insurer names: Aviva, AXA, Zurich, Allianz, Hiscox, NFU Mutual. Patterns: 'AVIVA CLAIM PAYMENT', 'AXA INSURANCE CLAIM', 'HISCOX SETTLEMENT', 'CLAIM REF 123456'. Amounts: £500-£15,000. Frequency: irregular, one-off. Include claim reference numbers.

subcategories.name: Refunds Received
subcategories.definition: Refunds or credits received from suppliers or service providers, representing reversal of prior expense payments.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK refund receipts. Patterns: 'REFUND FROM [SUPPLIER]', 'CREDIT NOTE', 'SUPPLIER REFUND', 'OVERPAYMENT RETURN', 'REFUND - [DESCRIPTION]'. Supplier names: mix of utilities (British Gas, EDF), telecoms (BT, Vodafone), retail (Amazon, Screwfix). Amounts: £20-£800. Frequency: irregular. Should reference original purchase occasionally.

subcategories.name: Asset Sale Proceeds
subcategories.definition: Proceeds from selling business equipment, vehicles, or other assets. Treated as non-trading capital inflow.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK asset sale receipts. Patterns: 'VEHICLE SALE', 'EQUIPMENT SALE', 'ASSET DISPOSAL', 'SALE OF [ASSET TYPE]'. Asset types: van, car, machinery, equipment, furniture. Buyers: mix of business names and 'WEBUYANYCAR', 'AUTOTRADER', private buyers. Amounts: £1,000-£15,000. Frequency: very irregular, one-off.

COST_OF_SALES.name: Cost of Sales
COST_OF_SALES.definition: Direct costs required to produce, purchase, or deliver the goods or services sold. Used to calculate gross margin and assess production efficiency.

subcategories.name: Inventory and Wholesale
subcategories.definition: Purchases of stock, inventory, or wholesale goods intended for resale. Includes trade suppliers and wholesalers.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK wholesale and inventory purchases. Supplier names: Booker, Costco, Makro, Brakes, Bidfood, trade suppliers, manufacturers. Patterns: 'BOOKER LTD', 'COSTCO WHOLESALE', 'PAYMENT TO [SUPPLIER]', 'TRADE ACCOUNT'. Amounts: £200-£8,000 per transaction. Frequency: weekly or bi-weekly. Include account references occasionally: 'ACC 12345'.

subcategories.name: Raw Materials and Inputs
subcategories.definition: Inputs consumed to produce goods or deliver services including ingredients, consumables, and production materials.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK raw material purchases. Supplier types: ingredient suppliers, packaging suppliers, manufacturing inputs. Patterns: 'SUPPLIER INVOICE', 'MATERIALS PURCHASE', payments to trade suppliers. Supplier names: industry-specific (e.g., 'PACKAGING SUPPLIES LTD', 'INGREDIENT CO', 'MATERIALS DIRECT'). Amounts: £100-£5,000. Frequency: weekly to monthly.

subcategories.name: Subcontractors
subcategories.definition: Payments to subcontractors, freelancers, or third parties for outsourced work that directly supports revenue generation. Includes direct labour and subcontracted fulfilment.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK subcontractor payments. Patterns: 'SUBCONTRACTOR PAYMENT', 'FREELANCER', payment to individual names or trade businesses, 'CONTRACT WORK'. Payee names: mix of personal names (J SMITH PLUMBING, SARAH JONES DESIGN) and trade businesses (ABC BUILDERS, XYZ ELECTRICAL). Amounts: £200-£5,000. Frequency: weekly to monthly. Include 'CIS' occasionally for construction.

subcategories.name: Shipping and Fulfilment
subcategories.definition: Direct delivery and fulfilment costs tightly linked to sales volume, excluding general logistics overhead.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK shipping and fulfilment costs. Carriers: Royal Mail, DPD, Hermes/Evri, Yodel, Amazon Logistics, DHL, Parcelforce. Patterns: 'ROYAL MAIL', 'DPD UK', 'EVRI DELIVERY', 'AMAZON LOGISTICS', 'SHIPPING CHARGE'. Amounts: £5-£500 per transaction, can be daily for high-volume ecommerce. Frequency: daily to weekly. Include tracking references occasionally.

subcategories.name: Platform and Marketplace Fees
subcategories.definition: Marketplace or platform commissions and fees that act like cost of sales, distinct from payment processing fees.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK marketplace commission charges. Platforms: Amazon, eBay, Etsy, Deliveroo, Uber Eats, Just Eat. Patterns: 'AMAZON SELLER FEES', 'EBAY FINAL VALUE FEE', 'ETSY TRANSACTION FEE', 'DELIVEROO COMMISSION', 'JUST EAT SERVICE CHARGE'. Amounts: £10-£2,000 depending on sales volume. Frequency: monthly or per transaction batch.

OPERATING_EXPENSES.name: Operating Expenses
OPERATING_EXPENSES.definition: Day-to-day costs required to operate the business excluding direct production costs, people costs, finance costs, transport, and insurance. Supports operating leverage analysis.

subcategories.name: Rent and Rates
subcategories.definition: Commercial rent, business rates, and property-related occupancy costs for business premises.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK rent and rates payments. Patterns: 'RENT PAYMENT', 'COMMERCIAL RENT', 'BUSINESS RATES', payment to landlords or councils. Payee names: '[LANDLORD NAME]', '[COUNCIL NAME] BUSINESS RATES', 'PROPERTY MANAGEMENT LTD'. Council names: realistic UK councils (Westminster, Birmingham, Manchester, Leeds Council). Amounts: £800-£5,000 for rent, £200-£2,000 for rates. Frequency: monthly standing orders or quarterly for rates.

subcategories.name: Utilities
subcategories.definition: Electricity, gas, water, and other essential utility charges for business premises.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK utility payments. Providers: British Gas, EDF Energy, E.ON, Scottish Power, Octopus Energy, Thames Water, Severn Trent, United Utilities. Patterns: 'BRITISH GAS', 'EDF ENERGY', 'WATER BILL', 'GAS DIRECT DEBIT', 'ELECTRICITY PAYMENT'. Amounts: £50-£800 per month. Frequency: monthly direct debits. Include account references occasionally.

subcategories.name: Software and Subscriptions
subcategories.definition: Software, digital tools, SaaS products, licences, and recurring subscriptions supporting business operations.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK SaaS and software subscriptions. Providers: Microsoft, Adobe, Salesforce, Xero, QuickBooks, Sage, Google Workspace, Zoom, Slack, Dropbox, Shopify, Mailchimp. Patterns: 'MICROSOFT 365', 'ADOBE CREATIVE CLOUD', 'XERO SUBSCRIPTION', 'SAGE ACCOUNTING', 'GOOGLE WORKSPACE', 'ZOOM.US'. Amounts: £10-£500 per month. Frequency: monthly recurring payments. Include subscription tiers occasionally.

subcategories.name: Telecoms and Connectivity
subcategories.definition: Internet, phone, mobile contracts, connectivity services, and related communications expenses.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK telecoms payments. Providers: BT, Vodafone, EE, Three, O2, Sky Business, TalkTalk, Virgin Media. Patterns: 'BT BUSINESS', 'VODAFONE', 'EE MOBILE', 'O2 UK', 'SKY BUSINESS BROADBAND', 'VIRGIN MEDIA BUSINESS'. Amounts: £30-£300 per month. Frequency: monthly direct debits. Include account or phone numbers occasionally.

subcategories.name: Accounting and Legal
subcategories.definition: Fees for accounting, bookkeeping, audit, legal services, and tax advisory.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK accounting and legal fees. Service providers: accountant names (SMITH & CO ACCOUNTANTS, ABC ACCOUNTING), law firms (XYZ SOLICITORS, LEGAL SERVICES LTD), 'BOOKKEEPING SERVICES'. Patterns: 'ACCOUNTANT FEE', 'LEGAL FEES', 'AUDIT SERVICES', 'TAX ADVICE', 'BOOKKEEPING'. Amounts: £100-£2,000 for accounting, £200-£5,000 for legal. Frequency: monthly for bookkeeping, quarterly for accounting, ad-hoc for legal.

subcategories.name: Consulting and Advisory
subcategories.definition: Specialist consulting, business advisory, recruitment, and outsourced operational support services.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK consulting and advisory fees. Patterns: 'CONSULTANT FEE', 'BUSINESS ADVISOR', 'MANAGEMENT CONSULTANT', 'RECRUITMENT FEE', 'HR SERVICES'. Provider names: consultancy firms, individual consultants. Amounts: £500-£5,000. Frequency: irregular, project-based. Include project references occasionally.

subcategories.name: Marketing and Advertising
subcategories.definition: Advertising, marketing platforms, agencies, promotions, and sales-related services excluding marketplace commissions.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK marketing spend. Platforms: Google Ads, Facebook/Meta, LinkedIn, TikTok, Twitter/X. Agencies: MARKETING AGENCY LTD, DIGITAL SERVICES. Patterns: 'GOOGLE ADS', 'FACEBOOK ADS', 'META PLATFORMS', 'LINKEDIN ADVERTISING', 'MARKETING SERVICES', 'PROMOTIONAL MATERIALS'. Amounts: £50-£3,000 per month. Frequency: monthly for platforms, ad-hoc for agencies.

subcategories.name: Office and Administration
subcategories.definition: General administrative expenses including office supplies, stationery, postage, printing, and routine admin services.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK office and admin expenses. Suppliers: Amazon Business, Viking Direct, Staples, Office Outlet, Royal Mail, local printers. Patterns: 'AMAZON BUSINESS', 'OFFICE SUPPLIES', 'STATIONERY', 'PRINTING', 'POSTAGE', 'VIKING DIRECT'. Amounts: £20-£400. Frequency: monthly to irregular. Include item descriptions occasionally.

subcategories.name: Repairs and Maintenance
subcategories.definition: Maintenance and repair of premises, equipment, and operational assets excluding vehicle-specific running costs.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK repairs and maintenance. Service providers: MAINTENANCE SERVICES LTD, REPAIR CO, ABC PLUMBING, XYZ ELECTRICAL, PROPERTY MAINTENANCE. Patterns: 'REPAIRS', 'MAINTENANCE', 'PLUMBING', 'ELECTRICAL WORK', 'BUILDING REPAIR'. Amounts: £100-£2,000. Frequency: irregular, ad-hoc. Include job references occasionally.

subcategories.name: Equipment and Tools
subcategories.definition: Small equipment purchases, tools, consumables, and operational assets excluding major capital expenditure.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK equipment and tools purchases. Suppliers: Screwfix, Toolstation, Machine Mart, RS Components, Amazon, trade suppliers. Patterns: 'SCREWFIX', 'TOOLSTATION', 'EQUIPMENT PURCHASE', 'TOOLS', 'MACHINE MART'. Amounts: £30-£1,500 per transaction. Frequency: irregular. Should be small operational purchases, not vehicles or major machinery.

subcategories.name: Training and Memberships
subcategories.definition: Staff training courses, professional memberships, certifications, and industry association fees.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK training and membership fees. Organisations: professional bodies (ACCA, CIMA, ICE, RICS, BCS), training providers, industry associations. Patterns: 'TRAINING COURSE', 'PROFESSIONAL MEMBERSHIP', 'CERTIFICATION FEE', 'INDUSTRY ASSOCIATION', 'CPD TRAINING'. Amounts: £50-£1,500. Frequency: annual for memberships, irregular for training.

subcategories.name: Business Travel and Accommodation
subcategories.definition: Business travel costs including rail, taxis, flights, hotels, and subsistence excluding delivery logistics.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK business travel expenses. Providers: Trainline, LNER, GWR, Virgin Trains, Uber, Bolt, Premier Inn, Travelodge, British Airways, easyJet. Patterns: 'TRAINLINE', 'LNER TICKET', 'UBER TRIP', 'PREMIER INN', 'FLIGHT BOOKING', 'HOTEL'. Amounts: £10-£500 per trip. Frequency: irregular. Include journey details occasionally: 'LONDON TO MANCHESTER'.

PEOPLE_COSTS.name: People Costs
PEOPLE_COSTS.definition: Costs associated with employing and supporting staff including salaries, wages, statutory contributions, contractors, and benefits. Critical for people intensity metrics.

subcategories.name: Salaries and Wages
subcategories.definition: Regular wage and salary payments to employees including monthly salaries and weekly wages.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK payroll payments. Patterns: 'SALARY', 'WAGES', 'PAYROLL', payment to employee names, 'BACS SALARY PAYMENT', 'NET PAY'. Payee names: realistic employee names (J SMITH, SARAH JONES, M PATEL). Amounts: £1,200-£4,500 per employee per month for salaries, £300-£800 per week for wages. Frequency: monthly for salaries, weekly/fortnightly for wages. Include payroll references occasionally.

subcategories.name: Employer Taxes and Contributions
subcategories.definition: Employer National Insurance contributions and pension contributions.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK employer NI and pension payments. Patterns: 'EMPLOYER NI', 'NATIONAL INSURANCE', 'PENSION CONTRIBUTION', 'NEST PENSION', 'EMPLOYER PENSION', payments to pension providers (NEST, NOW Pensions, Scottish Widows, Aviva). Amounts: £200-£1,500 per month depending on payroll size. Frequency: monthly. Include pension scheme references.

subcategories.name: Contractors and Freelancers
subcategories.definition: Payments to contractors, freelancers, consultants, and agency labour used as workforce capacity, excluding subcontractors directly tied to COGS.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK contractor payments. Patterns: 'CONTRACTOR', 'FREELANCER', payment to individual names or limited companies, 'CONSULTANCY FEE', 'AGENCY WORKER'. Payee names: individual names with Ltd suffix (J SMITH LTD, SARAH JONES CONSULTING LTD), recruitment agencies (REED, HAYS, MANPOWER). Amounts: £500-£4,000. Frequency: monthly or project-based. Include 'IR35' context occasionally.

subcategories.name: Staff Expenses and Reimbursements
subcategories.definition: Expense reimbursements to employees including travel claims, subsistence, and allowable business expenses repaid to staff.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK staff expense reimbursements. Patterns: 'EXPENSE CLAIM', 'STAFF REIMBURSEMENT', payment to employee names with 'EXPENSES' suffix, 'TRAVEL CLAIM', 'MILEAGE CLAIM'. Amounts: £30-£400 per claim. Frequency: monthly or ad-hoc. Include expense reference codes occasionally: 'EXP-2024-001'.

subcategories.name: Benefits and Wellbeing
subcategories.definition: Employee benefits, healthcare contributions, wellness programs, and staff-related spend funded by the business.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK employee benefits. Providers: Bupa, AXA Health, Vitality, Cycle to Work schemes. Patterns: 'BUPA HEALTHCARE', 'AXA HEALTH INSURANCE', 'EMPLOYEE BENEFITS', 'WELLNESS SCHEME', 'CYCLE SCHEME'. Amounts: £50-£500 per month. Frequency: monthly. Include employee counts occasionally.

FINANCE_COSTS.name: Finance and Banking Costs
FINANCE_COSTS.definition: Costs associated with accepting payments, banking services, and borrowing. Used to calculate finance cost burden ratio.

subcategories.name: Card Processing Fees
subcategories.definition: Fees charged by payment processors and acquirers for card acceptance and settlement services.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK card processing fees. Processors: Worldpay, Stripe, Square, SumUp, PayPal, Barclaycard. Patterns: 'WORLDPAY FEES', 'STRIPE FEES', 'CARD PROCESSING CHARGE', 'MERCHANT SERVICE CHARGE', 'TRANSACTION FEE', 'SQUARE FEES'. Amounts: £20-£800 per month depending on card volume. Frequency: monthly or per batch. Include percentage rates occasionally: '1.75% + 20p'.

subcategories.name: Bank Charges and Fees
subcategories.definition: Account servicing charges, transaction fees, overdraft fees, and bank penalty charges.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK bank fees. Banks: Barclays, HSBC, Lloyds, NatWest, Santander, Metro Bank, Starling, Tide. Patterns: 'BANK CHARGES', 'ACCOUNT FEE', 'OVERDRAFT FEE', 'TRANSACTION CHARGE', 'UNPAID ITEM FEE', 'MONTHLY SERVICE FEE'. Amounts: £5-£150 per month, £20-£50 for overdraft fees. Frequency: monthly for service fees, ad-hoc for penalties.

subcategories.name: Loan Interest and Finance Charges
subcategories.definition: Interest payments and finance charges on loans, overdrafts, credit facilities, and asset finance.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK interest charges. Patterns: 'LOAN INTEREST', 'OVERDRAFT INTEREST', 'FINANCE CHARGE', 'INTEREST ON LOAN', '[LENDER] INTEREST'. Lenders: high street banks, alternative lenders (Funding Circle, iwoca, Bibby Financial). Amounts: £50-£2,000 per month. Frequency: monthly. Include loan reference numbers occasionally.

subcategories.name: FX and Cross-Border Fees
subcategories.definition: Foreign exchange charges and cross-border transaction fees charged by banks or payment providers.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK FX and cross-border fees. Patterns: 'FX FEE', 'FOREIGN EXCHANGE CHARGE', 'INTERNATIONAL PAYMENT FEE', 'CURRENCY CONVERSION', 'CROSS BORDER CHARGE'. Amounts: £5-£100 per transaction. Frequency: irregular, linked to international transactions. Include currency codes occasionally: 'EUR', 'USD'.

ASSETS_AND_CAPITAL.name: Assets and Capital
ASSETS_AND_CAPITAL.definition: Balance sheet movements including financing structure, liquidity management, asset purchases, and owner equity activity. Excluded from operating margin calculations.

subcategories.name: Internal Transfers
subcategories.definition: Transfers between accounts owned by the same business including treasury sweeps and internal reallocations.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK inter-account transfers. Patterns: 'TRANSFER TO [ACCOUNT NAME]', 'TRANSFER FROM [ACCOUNT NAME]', 'INTERNAL TRANSFER', 'SWEEP TO SAVINGS', 'FUNDS TRANSFER'. Account names: 'BUSINESS CURRENT ACCOUNT', 'RESERVE ACCOUNT', 'SAVINGS ACCOUNT'. Amounts: £500-£50,000. Frequency: irregular. Both DEBIT (transfer out) and CREDIT (transfer in) transactions.

subcategories.name: Savings and Deposits
subcategories.definition: Transfers into or out of deposit, notice, or savings products indicating liquidity buffering behaviour.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK savings movements. Banks: same as current account providers. Patterns: 'TRANSFER TO SAVINGS', 'DEPOSIT ACCOUNT TRANSFER', 'NOTICE ACCOUNT', 'WITHDRAWAL FROM SAVINGS'. Amounts: £1,000-£30,000. Frequency: irregular, strategic. Both DEBIT (deposit into savings) and CREDIT (withdrawal from savings).

subcategories.name: Loan and Credit Drawdowns
subcategories.definition: Inflows from loans, revolving credit, merchant cash advance, invoice finance, or other borrowing.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK loan drawdowns. Lenders: high street banks (Barclays, HSBC, Lloyds), alternative lenders (Funding Circle, iwoca, Tide, Capify, MarketFinance). Patterns: 'LOAN ADVANCE', 'CREDIT FACILITY DRAWDOWN', 'MERCHANT CASH ADVANCE', 'INVOICE FINANCE', 'REVOLVING CREDIT'. Amounts: £5,000-£100,000. Frequency: one-off or quarterly. Include facility references.

subcategories.name: Loan Repayments
subcategories.definition: Principal repayments for loans and credit facilities, excluding interest where separately identifiable.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK loan repayments. Patterns: 'LOAN REPAYMENT', 'CREDIT REPAYMENT', 'PRINCIPAL PAYMENT', '[LENDER] LOAN', 'DD LOAN REPAYMENT'. Lenders: same as drawdown. Amounts: £200-£5,000 per month. Frequency: monthly direct debits. Include loan account references.

subcategories.name: Lease Payments
subcategories.definition: Asset finance lease payments and hire purchase instalments for vehicles, equipment, and machinery.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK lease payments. Providers: Close Brothers, Lombard, Hitachi Capital, Black Horse, Lex Autolease. Patterns: 'LEASE PAYMENT', 'HP PAYMENT', 'VEHICLE FINANCE', 'EQUIPMENT LEASE', '[PROVIDER] FINANCE'. Amounts: £150-£1,500 per month. Frequency: monthly direct debits. Include asset type occasionally: 'VAN LEASE', 'EQUIPMENT HP'.

subcategories.name: Asset Purchases
subcategories.definition: One-off or infrequent purchases of long-lived assets such as machinery, vehicles, fit-outs, or major equipment representing capital expenditure.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK capital expenditure. Patterns: 'VEHICLE PURCHASE', 'EQUIPMENT PURCHASE', 'MACHINERY', payments to dealers or suppliers, 'CAPITAL INVESTMENT'. Sellers: vehicle dealers, equipment suppliers, commercial vehicle sellers. Amounts: £3,000-£40,000. Frequency: very irregular, one-off. Include asset description.

subcategories.name: Owner Investment and Capital Injections
subcategories.definition: Capital introduced by owners or directors into the business, including shareholder funding and director loans in.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK owner capital injections. Patterns: 'DIRECTOR LOAN', 'CAPITAL INJECTION', 'OWNER INVESTMENT', 'SHAREHOLDER FUNDING', payment from director names. Director names: realistic personal names. Amounts: £1,000-£50,000. Frequency: irregular, strategic. Include 'DIRECTOR' in description.

subcategories.name: Owner Drawings and Dividends
subcategories.definition: Funds extracted by owners including personal transfers out, drawings, dividend distributions, and director loan repayments.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK owner withdrawals. Patterns: 'DRAWINGS', 'DIVIDEND PAYMENT', 'TRANSFER TO [DIRECTOR NAME]', 'DIRECTOR LOAN REPAYMENT', 'OWNER WITHDRAWAL'. Payee names: personal names or 'PERSONAL ACCOUNT'. Amounts: £500-£10,000. Frequency: monthly or irregular. Label clearly as personal/director transactions.

TAX_AND_STATUTORY.name: Tax and Statutory Payments
TAX_AND_STATUTORY.definition: Statutory payments and fees required by government bodies including taxes, duties, licensing fees, and penalties.

subcategories.name: VAT Payments
subcategories.definition: VAT or sales tax payments to HMRC.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK VAT payments to HMRC. Patterns: 'VAT PAYMENT', 'HMRC VAT', 'VAT RETURN PAYMENT', 'VALUE ADDED TAX'. Amounts: £500-£15,000 quarterly. Frequency: quarterly. Include VAT period references: 'VAT Q1 2024', 'PERIOD 03/24'.

subcategories.name: Corporation Tax
subcategories.definition: Corporation tax and business tax payments to HMRC.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK corporation tax payments. Patterns: 'CORPORATION TAX', 'HMRC CT PAYMENT', 'COMPANY TAX', 'CT600 PAYMENT'. Amounts: £1,000-£25,000 annually. Frequency: typically annual or semi-annual. Include accounting period references.

subcategories.name: PAYE and Payroll Taxes
subcategories.definition: PAYE income tax and employee National Insurance payments to HMRC.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK PAYE payments. Patterns: 'PAYE PAYMENT', 'HMRC PAYE', 'PAYROLL TAX', 'EMPLOYEE TAX & NI', 'RTI PAYMENT'. Amounts: £500-£8,000 per month depending on payroll size. Frequency: monthly. Include tax month references: 'MONTH 12', 'TAX MONTH 03'.

subcategories.name: Licences and Permits
subcategories.definition: Government registrations, business licences, permits, and statutory fees.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK licensing fees. Patterns: 'LICENCE FEE', 'TRADE LICENCE', 'ALCOHOL LICENCE', 'PREMISES LICENCE', payments to local councils, 'REGISTRATION FEE', 'PERMIT'. Amounts: £100-£2,000. Frequency: annual or one-off. Include licence types: 'FOOD HYGIENE', 'ALCOHOL', 'TAXI LICENCE'.

subcategories.name: Fines and Penalties
subcategories.definition: Government or regulatory fines and penalties including HMRC penalties, parking fines, and compliance penalties.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK fines and penalties. Patterns: 'PENALTY CHARGE', 'HMRC PENALTY', 'LATE FILING PENALTY', 'PARKING FINE', council penalty notices. Amounts: £50-£1,000. Frequency: irregular, should be rare. Include penalty reasons occasionally: 'LATE VAT RETURN', 'PARKING PCN'.

INSURANCE.name: Insurance
INSURANCE.definition: Payments to insurers to transfer operational and business risks. Supports resilience and risk maturity assessment.

subcategories.name: Business Insurance
subcategories.definition: General business insurance, commercial packages, public liability, employers liability, and property insurance.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK business insurance premiums. Insurers: Aviva, AXA, Zurich, Allianz, Hiscox, NFU Mutual, Simply Business. Patterns: 'BUSINESS INSURANCE', 'COMMERCIAL INSURANCE', 'PUBLIC LIABILITY', 'EMPLOYERS LIABILITY', '[INSURER] PREMIUM'. Amounts: £50-£500 per month or £500-£3,000 annually. Frequency: monthly or annual. Include policy types.

subcategories.name: Vehicle Insurance
subcategories.definition: Commercial vehicle insurance for business vehicles, vans, and fleet cover.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK vehicle insurance. Insurers: Admiral, Direct Line, AXA, Aviva, NFU. Patterns: 'VEHICLE INSURANCE', 'VAN INSURANCE', 'FLEET INSURANCE', 'COMMERCIAL VEHICLE', '[INSURER] MOTOR'. Amounts: £80-£400 per month. Frequency: monthly or annual. Include vehicle registration occasionally.

subcategories.name: Professional Indemnity
subcategories.definition: Professional indemnity or equivalent cover for advice, professional services, and errors & omissions.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK professional indemnity insurance. Insurers: Hiscox, AXA, QBE, Markel. Patterns: 'PROFESSIONAL INDEMNITY', 'PI INSURANCE', 'ERRORS & OMISSIONS', '[INSURER] PI COVER'. Amounts: £100-£800 per year. Frequency: annual. Include profession occasionally: 'CONSULTANT PI', 'ACCOUNTANT PI'.

TRANSPORT_AND_LOGISTICS.name: Transport and Logistics
TRANSPORT_AND_LOGISTICS.definition: Operational transport and logistics costs for business operations, distinct from direct shipping costs in COGS and business travel in operating expenses.

subcategories.name: Fuel
subcategories.definition: Fuel purchases for business vehicles including petrol, diesel, and commercial fuel cards.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK fuel purchases. Suppliers: Shell, BP, Esso, Texaco, Tesco, Sainsbury's, Morrisons fuel. Fuel cards: Allstar, UK Fuels, Keyfuels, Shell Card. Patterns: 'SHELL [LOCATION]', 'BP PETROL', 'ALLSTAR FUEL CARD', 'KEYFUELS', 'FUEL PURCHASE'. Amounts: £30-£200 per fill, £200-£1,500 per month for fleet. Frequency: weekly to daily. Include location occasionally.

subcategories.name: Vehicle Running Costs
subcategories.definition: Vehicle servicing, repairs, MOT, road tax, tolls, parking, and routine running costs for business vehicles.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK vehicle costs. Patterns: 'MOT TEST', 'VEHICLE SERVICE', 'CAR REPAIR', 'DVLA ROAD TAX', 'PARKING CHARGE', 'CONGESTION CHARGE', 'TOLL PAYMENT', 'DARTFORD CROSSING'. Service providers: Halfords, Kwik Fit, local garages, councils for parking. Amounts: £50-£500. Frequency: irregular. Include vehicle registration occasionally.

subcategories.name: Courier and Delivery Services
subcategories.definition: Courier services and delivery partner fees for business operations, distinct from direct fulfilment costs in COGS.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK courier costs for general business use (not direct fulfilment). Carriers: DHL, UPS, FedEx, local couriers, CitySprint. Patterns: 'DHL EXPRESS', 'UPS COURIER', 'COURIER SERVICE', 'SAME DAY DELIVERY'. Amounts: £10-£150. Frequency: irregular. Should be general business courier use, not customer fulfilment.

subcategories.name: Vehicle Rental and Hire
subcategories.definition: Short-term vehicle rental and hire for business operations.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK vehicle rental. Providers: Enterprise, Hertz, Europcar, Sixt, Avis. Patterns: 'ENTERPRISE RENT', 'HERTZ RENTAL', 'VAN HIRE', 'VEHICLE RENTAL'. Amounts: £50-£400 per hire. Frequency: irregular, weekly/monthly rentals. Include hire period occasionally: 'WEEKLY HIRE', '3 DAY RENTAL'.

ADJUSTMENTS.name: Adjustments and Exceptions
ADJUSTMENTS.definition: Corrective, disputed, or non-recurring entries that should not distort core metrics including refunds, reversals, chargebacks, and failed payments.

subcategories.name: Refunds Issued
subcategories.definition: Refund payments made by the business back to customers, should be netted against revenue.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK customer refunds. Patterns: 'REFUND TO CUSTOMER', 'CUSTOMER REFUND', 'REFUND PAYMENT', 'RETURN REFUND', payment to customer names or 'REFUND'. Amounts: £10-£500. Frequency: irregular. Include order references occasionally: 'ORDER 12345 REFUND'.

subcategories.name: Chargebacks and Disputes
subcategories.definition: Chargebacks, payment disputes, and related reversals where identifiable.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK chargebacks. Patterns: 'CHARGEBACK', 'PAYMENT DISPUTE', 'DISPUTED TRANSACTION', 'REVERSAL - CHARGEBACK', '[ACQUIRER] CHARGEBACK'. Acquirers: Worldpay, Stripe, Barclaycard. Amounts: £20-£1,000. Frequency: irregular, should be rare. Include dispute reference codes.

subcategories.name: Reversals and Corrections
subcategories.definition: Bank reversals, corrections, and accounting adjustments that are not chargebacks.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK reversals and corrections. Patterns: 'REVERSAL', 'CORRECTION', 'BANK ERROR CORRECTION', 'ADJUSTMENT', 'CANCELLED TRANSACTION'. Can be DEBIT or CREDIT depending on original error. Amounts: £10-£2,000. Frequency: very rare. Include reference to original transaction occasionally.

subcategories.name: Failed and Returned Payments
subcategories.definition: Returned direct debits, failed payments, and returned transfers indicating operational friction or liquidity stress.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate UK failed payment returns. Patterns: 'DD RETURNED', 'UNPAID DIRECT DEBIT', 'FAILED PAYMENT RETURNED', 'REFER TO DRAWER', 'INSUFFICIENT FUNDS'. Amounts: match original payment being returned. Frequency: should be rare, indicates stress. Include reason codes: 'INDEMNITY CLAIM', 'NO FUNDS'.

UNCATEGORISED.name: Uncategorised
UNCATEGORISED.definition: Transactions with insufficient confidence to assign to a specific category. Flagged for manual review and excluded from automated credit metrics.

subcategories.name: Low Confidence Classification
subcategories.definition: Transaction descriptions that are too ambiguous or generic to classify with acceptable confidence. Requires human review.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate ambiguous UK transactions. Patterns: 'PAYMENT', 'TRANSFER', 'TRANSACTION', generic references with numbers only, unclear counterparties, 'DD PAYMENT 12345', 'FP RECEIVED', 'STANDING ORDER'. Amounts: variable. These should be genuinely unclear - missing merchant names, no context, just reference numbers.

subcategories.name: Apparent Personal Spend
subcategories.definition: Transactions that appear to be personal rather than business expenditure on a business account. Behavioural risk signal, excluded from operating costs.
subcategories.synthetic_prompt: Generate apparent personal spend on business accounts. Patterns: personal retail (Amazon personal items, supermarket grocery shops for home), entertainment (Netflix, Spotify, cinema, restaurants that seem personal), gambling (Betfair, Ladbrokes, online gaming), cash withdrawals. Suppliers: Netflix, Spotify, Amazon, supermarkets for non-business items. Amounts: £5-£200. Frequency: irregular. Should be clearly non-business in nature. Use sparingly and only for high-confidence personal spend.
